title: SSPs and Transaction Control
text: SSPs and Transaction Control By default, DBMS transaction control is not affected by the execution of a stored procedure. However, if transaction control is manipulated within a stored procedure, Uniface cannot guarantee a correct transaction state. You can use the commit and rollback Proc statements for DBMS transaction control. Multiple (parallel) transactions are supported. A stored procedure either runs in the same transaction as the calling Uniface component, or in the main transaction when called directly from a non-Uniface component. When the accessed entity is a Uniface Server, stored procedures are executed on the Uniface Server. This is because it must belong to the same transaction as the entity on the Uniface component. Global transactions (available via middleware modules ) are not supported. There is no support for NLS. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
